title: ardisia cincta
intro: Ardisia cincta, also known as the red ironbark or red iron tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating respiratory issues, and can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions. Ardisia cincta is often prepared in the form of a tea by infusing the roots or leaves in hot water, or as a decoction by simmering the plant material in water to release its active compounds.
